summary: Soundboard recording marred by backward‑sounding drums, yet it offers a clear early‑Bear glimpse on “Beat It On Down The Line.”
review: The set‑1 soundboard capture is usable but suffers from heavy noise‑reduction that makes the drum track sound reversed and muffles resonance. Musically the highlight noted is the opening “Beat It On Down The Line,” which gives a nice early glimpse of Jerry’s playing, while the missing second set limits further evaluation.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed review rates the show 3 stars, noting both positive (early Bear performance) and negative (drum artifacts) aspects; with limited data the overall rating stays around average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Noise‑reduction appears to have been applied, causing drums to sound backward and reducing instrument resonance; overall clarity is decent but not pristine.

standout_songs: Beat It On Down The Line
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is present with a strong opening; second set is missing from this release, limiting assessment of overall pacing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Early‑era playing praised on the opening track.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drum sound distorted by noise‑reduction, described as sounding backwards.
